title: T-054 — Prophet forecaster microservice and forecast actuals jobs (P2)
prompt:
# T-054 — Prophet forecaster microservice and forecast actuals jobs (P2)

PRD: `07-PLANNING-EXT-PRD.md` §7.3 Epic E3, §4.2 P2, §9.5-§9.6, §15.3.
UX/prototype refs are listed in `pipeline_inputs.scope_files`.
Project root: `/Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira`.

## Goal
Define the P2 ML forecast bridge implementation task.

## Implementation contract
Use internal Python Prophet service with health/retrain visibility. Scheduler UI must treat stale forecasts as warning, not a hard block.

ACP shape reminder: keep this task as one atomic implementation. Do not add ACP-owned fields. Keep local dependencies in `dependencies`; cross-module contracts in `cross_module_dependencies` are blockers/inputs, not ACP local task IDs.

pipeline_inputs.description: Implement P2 internal Prophet FastAPI service, daily retrain, inference, SMAPE actuals job and health endpoint.
pipeline_inputs.details: Internal-only service; no external managed ML APIs. Historical inputs can be internal wo_outputs or optional D365 sales history pull via integrations, but D365 remains optional and not P1 source of truth.

cross_module_dependencies.contract: PO generator consumes forecast output only after planner review.
Optional P2 D365 sales history pull; not required for P1.

acceptance_criteria: Given forecaster trains on 24 months of history, then demand_forecasts rows include yhat/lower/upper/model_version/confidence_score.
Given retrain fails or data is stale >7d, then health endpoint reports stale/unhealthy and scheduler can fall back to manual forecasts.
Given weekly actuals job runs, then forecast_actuals SMAPE is computed and alert threshold >30% is emitted.
test_strategy: RED service tests for train/infer/health and job tests for SMAPE formula.
Run pytest services/forecaster/tests and pnpm --filter web test forecast-actuals-job.
risk_red_lines: Do not call external ML APIs.
Do not block scheduler when forecaster is unhealthy.
Do not make D365 a hard dependency.
